stance: Risk-aware quality-at-reasonable-valuation; keep the structural compounders, lean the analyst-revision tailwind only via liquid quality, cut the deep-downgrade cyclical tail and nudge beta down.
experience_note: No last_round_review yet (early round) so nothing graded. What I am watching, pre-registered: (1) whether the high-ARM quality adds (ONGC, GRASIM, MUTHOOTFIN, LICI) outperform the deep-downgrade names I cut (ADANIENSOL, ADANIPORTS, BIOCON, VOLTAS) over the next 1-3 months — that tests whether ARM carries real short-horizon information here; (2) whether trimming the high-beta cyclical tail lowers realized portfolio beta toward 1.0 without giving up much return — tests my claim that part of historic excess was beta, not skill. I would change my mind if the cut-downgrade names rally hard while my ARM-quality adds stall (ARM mean-reverting against me), or if the structural large-caps I held through soft revisions keep de-rating — then the soft revisions were signal, not noise, and I over-anchored on franchise.
